museum_copy.title: @petersuber Solar Museum
museum_copy.subtitle: Community Archive proof-of-search map.
museum_copy.warning: Archive terrain, not reputation. Raw coverage ends where the archive/upload/API ends.

planets.name: Openaccess
planets.summary: Archive cluster around openaccess: 5730 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Openaccess receipt from 2018-09-10
artifacts.excerpt:
Scholars spend 68,500,000 hours/year performing peer review. https://t.co/tK37XRTMhT
The US federal minimum wage is $7.25/hour. Scholars deserve more than that, but take it as a starting point. That's a gift to publishers of at least $496,625,000/year in free labor.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: openaccess, here, update, see.

artifacts.title: Openaccess receipt from 2018-12-19
artifacts.excerpt:
The Max Planck Society (@maxplanckpress) is canceling its Elsevier subscriptions. The current agreement expires at the end of this month and will not be renewed.
https://t.co/E1yDlj1SKL
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: openaccess, here, update, see.

artifacts.title: Openaccess receipt from 2023-05-06
artifacts.excerpt:
This is big. No #embargoes. No #APCs. 

"The #EU is ready to agree that immediate #OpenAccess to…publicly funded research should become the norm, w/o authors having to pay fees & that the bloc should support #nonprofit scholarly publishing models."
https://t.co/0eI8FigzV6
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: openaccess, here, update, see.

artifacts.title: Openaccess receipt from 2018-09-12
artifacts.excerpt:
.@WellcomeTrust asked grant applicants whether it cd make their applications #openaccess. In the latest round, 87 of 97 said yes. Here they are.
https://t.co/G7fUOzLI1r
Next month Wellcome will annotate them & say why they did/didn't get funded. Very helpful to future applicants.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: openaccess, here, update, see.

artifacts.title: Open receipt from 2016-03-29
artifacts.excerpt: I'm happy to announce that my new book is out: Knowledge Unbound: Selected Writings on Open… https://t.co/GoS5zKlnRh https://t.co/30oGFPd8D9

artifacts.title: Open receipt from 2019-11-17
artifacts.excerpt:
1/ Kudos to the Italian Ministry of Public Education. Starting in January 2020, "only scientific papers published in open «repositories» will be evaluated and rewarded." [Google's English translation]. H/t Milica Ševkušić.
https://t.co/VQi9BazRrO
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: open, access, research, project.

artifacts.title: Open receipt from 2021-02-16
artifacts.excerpt:
New study: "Open data has significantly increased the citations of journal articles."
https://t.co/O9cdGbUE8t
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: open, access, research, project.

artifacts.title: Open receipt from 2018-03-24
artifacts.excerpt: Unfortunately nothing in this @ScienceEurope open letter on research infrastructure calls for *open* infrastructure, that is, #opensource and #nonprofit infrastructure. Without that, open research is at risk of corporate capture (like @ssrn & @bepress). https://t.co/SzFznWLhQ2
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: open, access, research, project.

planets.name: Research
planets.summary: Archive cluster around research: 420 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Research receipt from 2021-08-12
artifacts.excerpt:
One of those big steps that might be mistaken for a small step: “To help authors save time and effort in the article submission process, all @PLOS journals offer format-free initial submission for authors submitting research articles.”
https://t.co/qQ7FGRDb0o
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: research, data, update, journals.

artifacts.title: Research receipt from 2018-02-08
artifacts.excerpt: Bravo @research_uk: "Research councils consider the journal impact factor and metrics such as the H-index are not appropriate measures for assessing the quality of publications or...researchers, and...will not use [them] in our peer review processes." https://t.co/GXS3JTqPph
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: research, data, update, journals.

artifacts.title: Research receipt from 2020-02-02
artifacts.excerpt:
From @KCroweCBC: "When the story of the coronavirus (2019-nCOV) is finally written, it might well become a template for the utopian dream of #openscience — where research data is shared freely, unrestrained by competition, paywalls and patents."
https://t.co/GxdNlW39UX
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: research, data, update, journals.

artifacts.title: Research receipt from 2020-01-15
artifacts.excerpt:
New survey: "Only 14% of researchers agree that current metrics have had a positive impact on research culture, and 43% believe that their workplace puts more value on metrics than on research quality." #ReimagineResearch
https://t.co/790McfOuh4
(Lots of other impt findings too.)
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: research, data, update, journals.

planets.name: New
planets.summary: Archive cluster around new: 455 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: New receipt from 2020-09-20
artifacts.excerpt:
New study: "More than 90% of the scientific articles published by Colombian researchers are in English....Publishing in a 2d language creates additional financial costs...&...problems with reading comprehension, writing ease & time, & anxiety."
https://t.co/ZLDvIdNZSq
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: new, study, data, journals.

artifacts.title: New receipt from 2019-12-08
artifacts.excerpt:
New study: "We found that articles with a preprint had, on average, a 49% higher Altmetric Attention Score and 36% more citations than articles without a preprint."
https://t.co/PToQCknATP
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: new, study, data, journals.

artifacts.title: New receipt from 2019-09-28
artifacts.excerpt:
New study: The median studied author spends 52 hours/year formatting journal manuscripts, or $477/manuscript, or $1,908/person/year. Journals should eliminate "strict formatting guidelines, at least prior to acceptance."
https://t.co/mSh5SGQdJF
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: new, study, data, journals.

artifacts.title: New receipt from 2021-02-06
artifacts.excerpt:
Interesting new sign of shady journal practices: the Percentage of Papers by the Most Prolific author (PPMP). The higher, the worse. The median PMPP is 2.9%. The 95th percentile starts at 10.6%. Most of the MP authors were on the j's editorial board.
https://t.co/Q0l53tIEYz
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: new, study, data, journals.

planets.name: Access
planets.summary: Archive cluster around access: 135 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Access receipt from 2019-01-06
artifacts.excerpt:
Elsevier charges non-subscribers $39.95 for 24 hrs of access to this 1-page, 3-paragraph letter to the editor. 
https://t.co/wED0WkWOe4
This is not rare. But we should flag examples now and then to keep it from becoming normal.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: access, public, update, free.

artifacts.title: Access receipt from 2019-01-09
artifacts.excerpt:
Summary of @LidiaBorrellDam findings: 27 European countries paid a total of €907,947,409 for 1 year's worth of access to paywalled subscriptions and electronic resources. 
https://t.co/DtmauFm1x2
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: access, public, update, free.

artifacts.title: Access receipt from 2019-03-14
artifacts.excerpt:
QUIZ. How long ago did the total human population reach the point at which *half* of us had internet access?

ANSWER. Late 2018. About three months ago. 

This and more eye-opening numbers from the @UN's @ITU.
https://t.co/mtV7SijUII
